name: Temporal Location Shift
description: Tests that NeuroMem tracks temporal validity — when a user moves cities, only the current location should be active.

conversation.content: Hey, I'm Priya. I currently live in San Francisco and work as a product manager.
Hi Priya! San Francisco is a great city for product management. What kind of products do you work on?
I have a cat named Luna and I really enjoy hiking on weekends.
Luna sounds lovely! SF has amazing hiking spots — Lands End and the Marin Headlands are gorgeous.
Big news — I just moved to Austin, Texas last week for a new opportunity!
Congratulations on the move to Austin! It's a booming tech hub. How are you settling in?
I started a new role at a startup called GreenLeaf here in Austin.
GreenLeaf sounds exciting! What does the startup focus on?

test_queries.description: Should return Austin, NOT San Francisco. Temporal validity must show only current location.
Should return the current employer GreenLeaf.

rag_expected_failure: Standard RAG retrieves both 'lives in San Francisco' and 'moved to Austin' with similar embeddings, causing hallucinated dual-location answers.
neuromem_advantage: Knowledge Graph valid_from/valid_to timestamps + Ebbinghaus decay on old location
